title: Designing a Writing Crew
concept: A worked design for a team of research, outline, drafting, and proofreading agents.

exercises.prompt: What process builds a shared list of tasks for team members to pull from?
exercises.explain: A shared task backlog allows team members to pull tasks dynamically based on their availability.

cards.title: Designing a Writing Crew
cards.body: A writing crew is like a team of specialized builders working together to create a strong written piece. Each member has a specific role to support the final product.

cards.title: Writing Crew
cards.body: A writing crew is a group of agents, each with distinct tasks like researching, outlining, drafting, and proofreading to collaboratively create content.

cards.title: Like Building a House
cards.body: Just as different specialists like architects, electricians, and plumbers contribute to building a house, writing requires multiple experts to craft, refine, and finalize a piece.

cards.title: Team Roles
cards.body: In a writing crew, one agent might research facts, another outlines the structure, one drafts the text, and a final agent proofs the work. Each plays a vital part.

cards.title: Myth: Only One Writer is Needed
cards.body: Many believe a single writer can do it all, but teamwork in writing brings diverse skills and perspectives that enhance quality.

cards.title: Writing Crew Structure
cards.body: A clear structure helps the writing crew work effectively together.

exercises.prompt: What role does the outline agent play in a writing crew?
exercises.explain: The outline agent organizes the structure, helping the crew know how to build the content.

exercises.prompt: True or False: Each agent in the writing crew performs the same tasks.
exercises.explain: False, because each agent has a different role, making teamwork essential.

exercises.prompt: One important task of the research agent is to gather __________.
exercises.sentence: One important task of the research agent is to gather information.
exercises.explain: The research agent's job is to gather information to inform the writing process.

exercises.prompt: Which of these roles does NOT belong in a writing crew?
exercises.explain: A carpenter is not part of a writing crew; the crew consists of writing-specific roles.

exercises.prompt: Which sequence of tasks outlines how a writing crew should ideally work together?
exercises.explain: The correct order is researching first to gather information, then outlining to organize ideas, followed by drafting the text, and finally proofreading to correct errors.
